$note: Trigger-reliability fixture for SKILL.md's frontmatter description. Method: an LLM judge sees ONLY the frontmatter (name + description) as it appears in an available-skills list, plus each prompt in isolation, and decides whether a competent agent would invoke the skill before proceeding. Not a CI gate (no LLM in CI) — re-run manually whenever the description changes, and record the run below. A miss on any 'expect: true' prompt means the description has a coverage hole; a false-positive on 'expect: false' means it over-triggers and will annoy users into disabling it.
description_under_test: Use BEFORE picking a tool, model, or effort level for generation or delegation work — generating an image, animating a frame, reconning a codebase, researching the web, delegating implementation, getting a second-opinion review, digesting bulk material — and whenever asked which AI model/CLI to use, what it costs, whether a lane (codex, gemini, grok, claude subagents) is installed/authenticated/alive, or how much quota remains.

prompts.prompt: generate a hero image for the landing page
can you animate this product shot into a short clip?
have another model double-check this diff before I merge
which AI CLI should I use to summarize this 3-hour meeting recording?
delegate implementing this parser to a cheaper model
is codex installed here and do I have quota left?
research the best vector DB options on the web and cite sources
what's the cheapest way to bulk-read these 500 files and answer questions?
make a sticker sheet design for print-on-demand
how much of my claude weekly limit have I used?
fix this failing unit test
rename variable foo to bar across the repo
what does this regex do?
write a README for this project
rebase my branch onto main
why is this docker build slow?
add error handling to this function
explain the difference between TCP and UDP
update the changelog for v2.1
profile this python script for hotspots

runs.judge: claude sonnet-5 (single judge, prompts judged in isolation within one run)
runs.notes: First measurement, taken against the v2.1 trigger-grammar rewrite of the description. Single-judge single-run — treat as a smoke signal, not a benchmark.
